behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/orchestration -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function could miss wiring omissions across modules (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required because source modules can be in-process fakes returning representative payloads (REAL-INFRA no). LLM output quality is not under test; only the input assembled before generation (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System because behavior text focuses on "content passed" rather than real endpoint/network behavior. Rejected Agentic because behavior does not assess what the model generates, only what it receives.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with three stubbed source adapters and a capturing summary adapter; assert captured summary input contains fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log in one run.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source failure -> report composer), so unit is too low for the real handoff failure mode (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network is required to reproduce: one adapter can deterministically raise an unavailable error in-process (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model judgment quality; assertion is on fallback note and non-abort behavior (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System because behavior text is semantic unavailability handling, not transport-specific failure under real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source stubs returning data and one source stub raising an unavailable exception/timeout type; assert final report includes a `data unavailable` section marker for that source and overall generation completes.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary output quality (faithfulness to key values), which is model-judgment dependent and can vary run to run (LLM-DEP yes). Stubs would hide real failures by returning fixed "correct" text (MOCK-HIDE yes). This is a single model decision stage, not a multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP no), so Workflow is not required. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly tests semantic accuracy of the generated narrative, not routing/retry/payload mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on controlled input datasets with known key values; evaluate whether outputs preserve critical numbers/facts (with rubric or checker), over repeated runs to detect faithfulness failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, which crosses process/network/external email service boundaries (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process mocks can falsely pass while masking SMTP/API auth, transport, formatting, and inbox-delivery issues (MOCK-HIDE yes). Agentic is not required as minimum because the asserted outcome is delivery to recipient inbox, not model output quality; summary content can be fixture-controlled for this test. Rejected Agentic based on behavior text "reaches ... inbox correctly" (delivery correctness boundary). Rejected Workflow because no multiple model decisions are required to satisfy the assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in a test environment against reachable test data endpoints and a real test mailbox/email service; assert one email is received by configured recipient with expected report attachment/body structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure spans real infrastructure boundaries: delivery failure handling plus durable local-file fallback and retry across scheduled runs (often separate process invocations). Real filesystem/process behavior is part of the risk (permissions, path, persistence), so System is the safer minimum (REAL-INFRA yes). Integration fakes can miss true persistence/scheduling boundary bugs (MOCK-HIDE yes). No dependence on model semantic quality (LLM-DEP no), and not a multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP no). Rejected Agentic because behavior text is transport-failure recovery and retry semantics, not summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run 1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; verify fallback report file is created on disk. Execute run 2 (next schedule simulation/new process) with email restored; verify retry sends previously generated report and fallback state is cleared/marked sent.
